meaning: n.<口>推,挤vt.<口>推挤,猛推,强使vi.推
examples: She gave Gracie a shove toward the house.
You have to give the door a shove or it won't close.
You could give him a shove.
But I am telling you, if push comes to shove, I can't say anything.
Smith is not the first person who wants to tell his former bosses to shove it.
